title: Gediminas
description:
Gediminas (c. 1275 – 1341) was Grand Duke of Lithuania and founder of the Gediminid dynasty. He ruled the state in the first half of the 14th century, strengthening its borders and developing its defence system.

Around 1323, by his order, a castle was built in the city of Lida. The fortress became an important defensive point against Teutonic Knight raids and helped control the border territories. Lida Castle was part of a chain of fortifications along the Neman River.

Under Gediminas, central authority was consolidated across the state. Castles served not only for defence but also as administrative centres. The Grand Duke sought to maintain relations with various peoples and faiths, and continuously improved his military forces.

He permanently established the state capital in the city of Vilnius. Gediminas died in December 1341. His rule had a profound influence on the development of the entire region.

Today a monument to Gediminas stands in Lida — a tribute to the prince who left an enduring mark on the city's history.
